 {"id":"bd-170","title":"Export: Register export and import commands in main()","description":"# Register export and import Commands

## Task

Add export and import to the command dispatch in main().

## Implementation

Add to the case statement in main():

```bash
case "$command" in
    backup|b)
        backup_all "$@"
        ;;
    restore|r)
        if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
            log_error "Usage: asb restore <agent> [commit]"
            exit 1
        fi
        restore_agent "$@"
        ;;
    export|ex)
        if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
            log_error "Usage: asb export <agent> [file]"
            exit 1
        fi
        local agent="$1"
        shift
        if ! agent_exists "$agent"; then
            log_error "Unknown agent: ${agent}"
            log_info "Run 'asb list' to see available agents"
            exit 1
        fi
        export_backup "$agent" "$@"
        ;;
    import|im)
        if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
            log_error "Usage: asb import <file>"
            exit 1
        fi
        import_backup "$@"
        ;;
    # ... rest of commands ...
esac
```

## Command Aliases

- `export` or `ex` for export
- `import` or `im` for import

Short aliases follow the pattern of other commands (backup/b, restore/r, etc.)

## Argument Validation

- export requires agent name, file is optional (defaults to timestamped name)
- import requires archive path
- Both support "-" for stdin/stdout","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.453450315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:18.309698091Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:18.309588985Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-170","depends_on_id":"bd-29l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:06.084345918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-170","depends_on_id":"bd-3jx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:06.258621043Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-170","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.464457247Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-18r","title":"Dry-run: Modify backup_agent for dry-run mode","description":"# Modify backup_agent for Dry-Run Mode

## Task

Add dry-run handling to backup_agent() function.

## Implementation

Add early return at the start of backup_agent():

```bash
backup_agent() {
    local agent="$1"
    local source
    local dest
    local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"

    source=$(get_agent_source "$agent")
    dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")

    if [[ ! -d "$source" ]]; then
        log_warn "${agent_name} not found at ${source}"
        return 1
    fi

    # === DRY RUN MODE ===
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would backup ${agent_name}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Source: ${source}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Destination: ${dest}"
        
        # Show what files would change
        if command_exists rsync; then
            local changes
            changes=$(rsync -a --dry-run --itemize-changes \
                --exclude='.git' \
                --exclude='.gitignore' \
                --exclude='*.log' \
                --exclude='cache/' \
                --exclude='Cache/' \
                --exclude='.cache/' \
                --exclude='*.sqlite3-wal' \
                --exclude='*.sqlite3-shm' \
                "$source/" "$dest/" 2>/dev/null | head -30)
            
            if [[ -n "$changes" ]]; then
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Files that would be synced:"
                echo "$changes" | while read -r line; do
                    echo "  $line"
                done
            else
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} No file changes detected"
            fi
        fi
        
        # Check if commit would be created
        if [[ "${ASB_AUTO_COMMIT:-true}" == "true" ]]; then
            if [[ -d "$dest/.git" ]]; then
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would create backup commit"
            else
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would initialize git repo and create initial commit"
            fi
        fi
        
        return 0
    fi
    # === END DRY RUN MODE ===

    # ... rest of existing implementation unchanged ...
}
```

## rsync Itemize Changes

The `--itemize-changes` flag produces output like:
```
>f..t...... settings.json      # file, timestamp changed
>f.st...... config.yaml        # file, size and timestamp changed  
.d..t...... projects/          # directory, timestamp changed
```

This gives users detailed insight into what would be synced.

## Edge Cases

- If destination doesn't exist yet, rsync will show all files as new
- We limit output to 30 lines to avoid overwhelming output
- We check if git repo exists to determine "would initialize" vs "would commit"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:29.973427440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:20.732360235Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:20.732246421Z","close_reason":"backup_agent dry-run already implemented; cleaned redundant is_dry_run check and used is_truthy for commit preview","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18r","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:29.988384823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-18r","depends_on_id":"bd-2lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:44.944634479Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1gy","title":"Manual Acceptance Testing: Human verification checklist","description":"# Manual Acceptance Testing: Human Verification Checklist

## Purpose
This bead tracks MANUAL acceptance testing that requires human judgment and verification. This complements the automated unit tests and E2E tests.

## When to Use This Checklist
- After all automated tests pass
- Before release
- When testing subjective qualities (UX, messaging clarity)

## Manual Verification Checklist

### User Experience

#### Configuration File UX
- [ ] `asb config init` output is clear and helpful
- [ ] `asb config show` is well-formatted and readable
- [ ] Error messages for invalid config are helpful

#### Dry-Run UX
- [ ] Dry-run output clearly indicates no changes made
- [ ] Preview shows enough detail to understand what WOULD happen
- [ ] Colors are helpful (not distracting)

#### Conflict Detection UX
- [ ] Preview of restore changes is easy to understand
- [ ] Deletions are CLEARLY marked (users must understand data loss)
- [ ] Confirmation prompt is unambiguous
- [ ] "y to proceed, any other key to cancel" is clear

#### Export/Import UX
- [ ] Archive filename is sensible (date-based)
- [ ] Import prompts before overwriting are clear
- [ ] Progress indication for large archives

#### Shell Completion UX
- [ ] Completion instructions in help are easy to follow
- [ ] Completions feel natural and responsive
- [ ] No unexpected completions

### Help Text Review
- [ ] All new commands documented in `asb --help`
- [ ] All new flags documented
- [ ] Examples are accurate and work
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors

### Edge Cases (Manual Verification)
- [ ] What happens with no backup directory?
- [ ] What happens with no git installed?
- [ ] What happens with read-only filesystem?
- [ ] What happens with very long filenames?
- [ ] What happens with special characters in filenames?
- [ ] What happens on Windows Subsystem for Linux?

### Backward Compatibility
- [ ] Existing backups from v0.1 still work
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing commands
- [ ] Upgrade path is smooth

### Documentation Review
- [ ] README.md is accurate and complete
- [ ] All examples work as shown
- [ ] Installation instructions are correct
- [ ] Screenshots (if any) are up to date

## Test Environments
Test manually on:
- [ ] Ubuntu/Debian
- [ ] macOS (if available)
- [ ] Different terminal emulators

## Sign-off
- [ ] Developer self-review complete
- [ ] All automated tests passing
- [ ] All manual checks above verified
- [ ] Ready for release

## Notes
Automated tests are in separate beads:
- bd-3ig: Test Infrastructure
- bd-9rp: Unit Test: Configuration File
- bd-3hq: Unit Test: Dry-Run Mode
- bd-shi: Unit Test: Conflict Detection
- bd-k47: Unit Test: Import/Export
- bd-34i: Unit Test: Shell Completion

 {"id":"bd-1lm","title":"Feature: Configuration File Support","description":"# Feature: Configuration File Support

## Problem Statement

Currently asb uses environment variables (ASB_BACKUP_ROOT, ASB_AUTO_COMMIT, ASB_VERBOSE) for
configuration. This approach has significant UX problems:

1. **Invisible**: Users can't easily see what's configured
2. **Transient**: Settings don't persist across shell sessions unless manually added to RC files
3. **Scattered**: Users forget where they set variables
4. **Not self-documenting**: No comments explaining options
5. **Not portable**: Can't easily transfer settings between machines

## Solution

Implement a configuration file at ~/.config/asb/config (XDG-compliant) that:
- Is sourced at startup (it's just bash, so compatible with env vars)
- Takes precedence over environment variables when both exist
- Is self-documenting with comments
- Can be created with `asb config init`
- Can be viewed with `asb config show`

## Technical Design

### Config File Location
```
${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/asb/config
```

### Config File Format (bash-sourceable)
```bash
# asb configuration file
# Generated by: asb config init
# Documentation: asb help

# Backup location (default: ~/.agent_settings_backups)
ASB_BACKUP_ROOT="$HOME/.agent_settings_backups"

# Auto-commit on backup (default: true)  
ASB_AUTO_COMMIT=true

# Verbose output (default: false)
ASB_VERBOSE=false

# Future: custom exclusion patterns, agent definitions, etc.
```

### New Functions

1. `get_config_dir()` - Returns XDG-compliant config directory
2. `get_config_file()` - Returns path to config file
3. `load_config()` - Sources config file if it exists
4. `init_config()` - Creates default config file with comments
5. `show_config()` - Displays current effective configuration

### New Subcommands

- `asb config` - Show current config (alias for `asb config show`)
- `asb config init` - Create default config file
- `asb config show` - Display effective settings
- `asb config edit` - Open config in $EDITOR (optional nice-to-have)

## Implementation Notes

- Config file is sourced AFTER constants but BEFORE command parsing
- Environment variables still work (for CI/scripts) but config file takes precedence
- If config file doesn't exist, behavior is unchanged (backward compatible)
- The config file itself should be valid bash to allow complex expressions

## Why This Is Foundational

Other features may want configuration options:
- Dry-run could have a default mode setting
- Shell completion might have customization
- Import/export could have default archive location

Having the config infrastructure first enables cleaner implementation of other features.

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Config file is created in correct XDG location
- [ ] Config file is sourced at startup
- [ ] Config values override defaults
- [ ] Env vars still work when config doesn't exist
- [ ] Config precedence: config file > env var > default
- [ ] `asb config init` is idempotent (doesn't overwrite existing)
- [ ] `asb config show` displays all effective settings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:21:38.822857328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:01:14.065352685Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:01:14.065220226Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lm","depends_on_id":"bd-3u9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:21:38.851021296Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1w7","title":"Feature: Backup integrity verification (asb verify)","description":"Add a verification command to check backup integrity.

## Proposed Command
```bash
asb verify [agent...]        # Verify backup integrity
asb verify --all             # Verify all backups
asb verify --repair          # Attempt to repair issues
```

## Verification Checks
1. Git repository integrity (git fsck)
2. .gitignore present and valid
3. At least one commit exists
4. HEAD is valid
5. No loose objects corruption
6. Compare backup against source (optional)

## Output
- Summary of verification status for each agent
- Warnings for potential issues
- Exit code for scripting (0 = all good, 1 = issues found)

## Use Cases
- Pre-export verification
- Post-import verification

## Task

```bash
show_config() {
    local config_file
    config_file=$(get_config_file)

    printf '\n'
    printf '%b\n' "${BOLD}asb Configuration${RESET}"
    printf '%s\n' "─────────────────────────────"
    printf '\n'

    if [[ -f "$config_file" ]]; then
        printf "Config file: %s\n" "$config_file"
    else
        printf "Config file: %s (not created)\n" "$config_file"
        log_info "Run 'asb config init' to create"
    fi
    printf '\n'

    # Show effective values
    printf '%b\n' "${BOLD}Effective Settings${RESET}"
    printf '%s\n' "─────────────────────────────"
    printf "%-20s %s\n" "ASB_BACKUP_ROOT:" "$(get_backup_root)"
    printf "%-20s %s\n" "ASB_AUTO_COMMIT:" "${ASB_AUTO_COMMIT:-true}"
    printf "%-20s %s\n" "ASB_VERBOSE:" "${ASB_VERBOSE:-false}"
    printf '\n'

    # Show source of each value (helpful for debugging)
    printf '%b\n' "${DIM}Source: config file > environment > default${RESET}"
}

 {"id":"bd-39b","title":"Feature: Shell Completion","description":"# Feature: Shell Completion

## Problem Statement

Without tab completion, users must:
- Remember all command names
- Remember all agent names
- Type everything manually
- Reference help constantly

This makes the tool feel unpolished and increases friction.

## Solution

Provide tab completion scripts for the three major shells:
- Bash
- Zsh
- Fish

## Technical Design

### User Experience

```bash
$ asb <TAB>
backup    config    diff      export    help      history   import    
init      list      restore   version

$ asb backup <TAB>
aider     amp       claude    cline     codex     cursor    factory   
gemini    opencode  windsurf

$ asb backup cl<TAB>
$ asb backup claude   # Completed!

$ asb restore claude <TAB>
abc1234   def5678   ghi9012   # Shows recent commits
```

### Completion Sources

1. **Commands**: Static list of all subcommands
2. **Agent names**: From AGENT_FOLDERS keys
3. **Commits**: Dynamic, from git log of agent's backup repo
4. **Files**: For import command, complete .tar.gz files

### New Subcommand

```bash
asb completion [bash|zsh|fish]
```

This outputs the completion script for the specified shell.

### Installation

Users install completion by adding to their shell RC:

**Bash** (~/.bashrc):
```bash
eval "$(asb completion bash)"
```

**Zsh** (~/.zshrc):
```bash
eval "$(asb completion zsh)"
```

**Fish** (~/.config/fish/config.fish):
```bash
asb completion fish | source
```

### Bash Completion Script

```bash
_asb_completions() {
    local cur="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"
    local prev="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD-1]}"
    
    local commands="backup restore export import list history diff init config help version"
    local agents="aider amp claude cline codex cursor factory gemini opencode windsurf"
    local config_cmds="init show"
    
    case "$prev" in
        asb)
            COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "$commands" -- "$cur"))
            ;;
        backup|b|restore|r|history|h|diff|d|export|ex)
            COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "$agents" -- "$cur"))
            ;;
        config|cfg)
            COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "$config_cmds" -- "$cur"))
            ;;
        import|im)
            # Complete tar.gz files
            COMPREPLY=($(compgen -f -X '!*.tar.gz' -- "$cur"))
            ;;
        *)
            # For restore command, complete commits
            if [[ "${COMP_WORDS[1]}" == "restore" ]] && [[ $COMP_CWORD -eq 3 ]]; then
                local agent="${COMP_WORDS[2]}"
                local backup_dir="$HOME/.agent_settings_backups/.${agent}"
                if [[ -d "$backup_dir/.git" ]]; then
                    local commits
                    commits=$(git -C "$backup_dir" log --oneline -10 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f1)
                    COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "$commits" -- "$cur"))
                fi
            fi
            ;;
    esac
}

complete -F _asb_completions asb
```

## Implementation Notes

### Keeping Agent List in Sync

The completion scripts hardcode the agent list. When a new agent is added:
1. Add to AGENT_FOLDERS in asb
2. Update completion scripts

Alternatively, completion scripts could call `asb list --agents-only` dynamically,
but this adds latency to every tab press. Static list is better.

### Dynamic Commit Completion

For `asb restore <agent> <TAB>`, we dynamically list recent commits.
This requires:
- Knowing the backup directory path
- Git access
- Limiting to recent commits (10-20) for performance

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Bash completion works
- [ ] Zsh completion works  
- [ ] Fish completion works
- [ ] Commands complete correctly
- [ ] Agent names complete correctly
- [ ] File completion works for import
- [ ] Commit completion works for restore
- [ ] Completion installation instructions in help","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:26:21.785322398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:01:00.585570089Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:01:00.585446056Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39b","depends_on_id":"bd-1lm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:31.582580519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39b","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:31.757914369Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39b","depends_on_id":"bd-3co","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:31.919959949Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39b","depends_on_id":"bd-3u9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:26:21.803659376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39b","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:32.081521498Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

            ;;

```

 {"id":"bd-9rp","title":"Unit Test: Configuration File Support tests","description":"# Unit Test: Configuration File Support

## Purpose
Create an automated test script (tests/test_config.sh) that verifies all configuration file functionality.

## Test Script Requirements

### Setup/Teardown
- Create isolated test environment with custom XDG_CONFIG_HOME
- Clean up test artifacts after each test
- Support --verbose flag for detailed logging

### Test Cases

1. **test_config_init_creates_file**
   - Run `asb config init`
   - Verify file created at expected location
   - Verify file has valid YAML structure
   - Verify default values present

2. **test_config_init_idempotent**
   - Create config with custom value
   - Run `asb config init` again
   - Verify original file NOT overwritten

3. **test_config_show_displays_all**
   - Create config with known values
   - Run `asb config show`
   - Verify all settings displayed

4. **test_config_precedence**
   - Set value in config file
   - Set same value via env var
   - Verify config file wins

5. **test_missing_config_graceful**
   - Ensure no config file exists
   - Run `asb backup --dry-run`
   - Verify no errors, defaults used

6. **test_xdg_config_home_respected**
   - Set custom XDG_CONFIG_HOME
   - Run `asb config init`
   - Verify file in custom location

7. **test_malformed_config_error**
   - Create invalid YAML config
   - Run `asb config show`

 {"id":"bd-g6l","title":"Dry-run: Add dry-run support to restore command","description":"# Add Dry-Run Support to Restore

## Task

Add dry-run handling to restore_agent() function.

## Implementation

In restore_agent(), add dry-run handling after validation:

```bash
restore_agent() {
    local agent="$1"
    local commit="${2:-HEAD}"
    local source
    local dest
    local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"

    source=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
    dest=$(get_agent_source "$agent")

    if [[ ! -d "$source/.git" ]]; then
        log_error "No backup found for ${agent_name}"
        return 1
    fi

    # === DRY RUN MODE ===
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would restore ${agent_name}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} From: ${source}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} To: ${dest}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Commit: ${commit}"
        
        # Show what would change
        if [[ -d "$dest" ]]; then
            log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Changes that would be applied:"
            # Create temp with backup state for comparison
            local temp_backup
            temp_backup=$(mktemp -d)
            trap "rm -rf '$temp_backup'" RETURN
            git -C "$source" archive "$commit" 2>/dev/null | tar -C "$temp_backup" -xf - 2>/dev/null
            
            diff -rq "$dest" "$temp_backup" \
                --exclude='.git' \
                --exclude='.gitignore' \
                2>/dev/null | head -20 || log_info "  (no differences or comparison unavailable)"
        else
            log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would create ${dest} with backup contents"
        fi
        
        return 0
    fi
    # === END DRY RUN MODE ===

    # ... rest of existing implementation ...
}
```

## Why This Matters

Restore is even MORE important to preview than backup:
- Restore overwrites current settings
- Wrong commit selection could lose recent changes
- Dry-run lets user verify they're restoring the right thing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:30.240620781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:23.299100084Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:23.299019573Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-g6l","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:30.256884094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-g6l","depends_on_id":"bd-2lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:45.285629690Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-k47","title":"Unit Test: Import/Export Archive tests","description":"# Unit Test: Import/Export Archives

## Purpose
Create an automated test script (tests/test_export.sh) that verifies archive creation and restoration.

## Test Script Requirements

### Setup/Teardown
- Create isolated backup directories with git history
- Clean up archive files after tests
- Support --verbose flag for detailed logging

### Test Cases

1. **test_export_creates_archive**
   - Setup backup with git history
   - Run `asb export agent`
   - Verify .tar.gz file created
   - Verify filename is timestamped

2. **test_export_custom_filename**
   - Run `asb export agent custom.tar.gz`
   - Verify custom.tar.gz created
   - Verify no timestamped file created

3. **test_export_includes_git**
   - Export backup
   - Extract archive to temp dir
   - Verify .git directory exists
   - Verify `git log` shows history

4. **test_export_to_stdout**
   - Run `asb export agent -`
   - Pipe to tar -tzf -
   - Verify valid tar output
   - Verify contains expected files

5. **test_import_restores_backup**
   - Export a backup
   - Delete original backup dir
   - Run `asb import archive.tar.gz`
   - Verify backup dir restored
   - Verify git history intact

6. **test_import_detects_existing**
   - Export backup
   - Run `asb import archive.tar.gz` (without deleting)
   - Verify prompt about existing backup
   - Verify stdin='n' cancels

7. **test_import_force_overwrites**
   - Setup existing backup
   - Create archive with different content
   - Run `asb --force import archive.tar.gz`
   - Verify overwritten without prompt

8. **test_export_dryrun**
   - Run `asb --dry-run export agent`
   - Verify NO archive created
   - Verify output shows what WOULD be created

9. **test_import_invalid_archive**
   - Create invalid tar file
   - Run `asb import invalid.tar.gz`
   - Verify helpful error message
   - Verify exit code non-zero

10. **test_export_nonexistent_agent**
    - Run `asb export nonexistent`
    - Verify helpful error message
    - Verify lists valid agents

## Archive Verification
- Use tar -tzf to list contents
- Verify .git/objects directory present
- Check file permissions preserved

## Logging Requirements
- Each test logs: START, PASS/FAIL, duration
- Log archive contents on verification
- Show size of archive created","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:31.836255005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:33:21.414343903Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:33:21.414263222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k47","depends_on_id":"bd-3ig","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:26.482449288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k47","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:26.693568016Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-qum","title":"Feature: Pre/Post Backup Hooks","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:08:25.785071923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:00:05.740155647Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-qum","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PURPOSE: Execute user-defined scripts before and after backup/restore operations.

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Enables countless customizations without bloating core
- Users can add notifications, cloud sync, custom validation
- Simple convention-based approach (run-parts style)
- Solves many feature requests at once

HOOK DIRECTORIES:
~/.config/asb/hooks/
  pre-backup.d/    - Run before any backup
  post-backup.d/   - Run after successful backup
  pre-restore.d/   - Run before any restore
  post-restore.d/  - Run after successful restore

HOOK EXECUTION:
- Scripts run in alphabetical order (01-notify.sh, 02-sync.sh)
- Scripts receive environment variables:
  ASB_AGENT=claude
  ASB_SOURCE=/home/user/.claude
  ASB_BACKUP_DIR=/home/user/.agent_settings_backups/.claude
  ASB_OPERATION=backup|restore
  ASB_COMMIT=abc1234 (for post-backup)
- Scripts must be executable
- Non-zero exit from pre-hook aborts operation
- Non-zero exit from post-hook logs warning but continues

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Add run_hooks() function using run-parts pattern
- Call in backup_agent and restore_agent
- Create hook directory structure in asb config init
- Document hook interface in README
- Add asb hooks --list to show active hooks

EXAMPLE USE CASES:
- Cloud sync: post-backup.d/sync-to-s3.sh
- Notifications: post-backup.d/notify-desktop.sh
- Validation: pre-restore.d/check-conflicts.sh
- Logging: post-backup.d/log-to-file.sh

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Hooks run in correct order
- Environment variables populated correctly
- Pre-hook failure aborts operation
- Post-hook failure logs warning only
- asb hooks --list works
- Tests cover hook execution","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:08:39Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-shi","title":"Unit Test: Conflict Detection tests","description":"# Unit Test: Conflict Detection on Restore

## Purpose
Create an automated test script (tests/test_conflict.sh) that verifies restore safety and conflict detection.

## Test Script Requirements

### Setup/Teardown
- Create isolated test directories
- Mock TTY/non-TTY environments
- Support --verbose flag for detailed logging

### Test Cases

1. **test_restore_shows_preview**
   - Create backup with files A, B, C
   - Create source with files A (modified), D
   - Run `asb restore agent` (capture output)
   - Verify preview shows:
     - A: will be overwritten
     - B, C: will be added
     - D: will be DELETED (critical!)

2. **test_restore_requires_confirmation**
   - Setup scenario with changes
   - Run `asb restore agent` with stdin='n'
   - Verify restore CANCELLED
   - Verify source dir UNCHANGED

3. **test_restore_confirm_yes**
   - Setup scenario with changes
   - Run `asb restore agent` with stdin='y'
   - Verify restore COMPLETED
   - Verify files match backup

4. **test_restore_enter_cancels**
   - Run `asb restore agent` with stdin='' (just Enter)
   - Verify restore CANCELLED
   - Verify source dir UNCHANGED

5. **test_force_skips_confirmation**
   - Setup scenario
   - Run `asb --force restore agent` (no stdin)
   - Verify restore COMPLETED immediately

6. **test_non_tty_without_force_error**
   - Run `asb restore agent </dev/null`
   - Verify helpful error message
   - Verify suggests --force flag
   - Verify exit code non-zero

7. **test_preview_formatting**
   - Test with many files
   - Verify colors (+ green, - red, ~ yellow)
   - Verify sorted output
   - Verify file counts in summary

8. **test_no_changes_scenario**
   - Setup source identical to backup
   - Run `asb restore agent`
   - Verify "Already up to date" message
   - Verify no confirmation needed

## TTY Mocking
Use `script` command or pty tools to test TTY behavior:
\`\`\`bash
# Force TTY
script -q -c "asb restore agent" /dev/null <<< "y"
# Force non-TTY
asb restore agent </dev/null
\`\`\`

## Logging Requirements

+# Validate tag name - only alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen allowed

+# Create a tag for a backup commit
+tag_backup() {
+    local agent="$1"

+    local dest
+
+    dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$dest/.git" ]]; then

+        return 1

+            json_error "Tag '${tagname}' already exists for ${agent}"
+        fi
+        log_error "Tag '${tagname}' already exists for ${agent_name}"
+        log_info "Use 'asb tag ${agent} --delete ${tagname}' to remove it first"

+
+    # Resolve commit reference
+    if ! git -C "$dest" rev-parse --verify "$commit" &>/dev/null; then

+            json_output "{\"agent\":\"${agent}\",\"name\":\"$(json_escape_string "$agent_name")\",\"tag\":\"$(json_escape_string "$tagname")\",\"commit\":\"${resolved_commit}\",\"dry_run\":true}"
+        else
+            log_info "[DRY RUN] Would tag ${agent_name} backup as '${tagname}' at commit ${commit}"

+# Resolve a tag name to a commit hash (for restore)
+resolve_tag_or_commit() {
+    local agent="$1"
+    local ref="$2"
+    local dest
+
+    dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$dest/.git" ]]; then
+        echo ""
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Try to resolve as a tag first, then as a commit
+    if git -C "$dest" rev-parse --verify "refs/tags/${ref}" &>/dev/null; then
+        git -C "$dest" rev-parse "refs/tags/${ref}"
+    elif git -C "$dest" rev-parse --verify "$ref" &>/dev/null; then
+        git -C "$dest" rev-parse "$ref"
+    else
+        echo ""
+        return 1
+    fi

+    if ! agent_exists "$agent"; then
+        if is_json_output; then
+            json_error "Unknown agent: ${agent}"
+        fi
+        log_error "Unknown agent: ${agent}"

+    # Parse subcommand
+    case "${1:-}" in

+            list_tags "$agent"

+        --delete|-d)
+            if [[ -z "${2:-}" ]]; then
+                if is_json_output; then

+                fi
+                log_error "Usage: asb tag <agent> --delete <tagname>"

+            if is_json_output; then

+            fi
+            log_error "Unknown option: ${1}"
+            return 1
+            ;;

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
+LIB_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/lib"
+if [[ "$SCRIPT_DIR" == */lib ]]; then
+    LIB_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR"
+fi
+
+source "${LIB_DIR}/logging.sh"
+source "${LIB_DIR}/assertions.sh"
+source "${LIB_DIR}/test_utils.sh"
+source "${LIB_DIR}/fixtures.sh"
+
+declare -F create_claude_fixture >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "create_claude_fixture not loaded" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+assert_json_valid() {
+    local input="$1"
+    skip_if_missing python3 "python3 required for JSON tests" || return $?
+    echo "$input" | python3 -c 'import json, sys; json.load(sys.stdin)'
+}
+
+test_json_list_output() {
+    create_claude_fixture
+    run_asb backup claude
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+
+    run_asb --json list
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+    assert_contains "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT" "\"agent\":\"claude\""
+    assert_json_valid "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT"
+}
+
+test_json_history_output() {
+    create_claude_fixture
+    run_asb backup claude
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+
+    run_asb --json history claude
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+    assert_contains "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT" "\"commits\""
+    assert_json_valid "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT"
+}
+
+test_json_verify_output() {
+    create_claude_fixture
+    run_asb backup claude
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+
+    run_asb --json verify claude
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+    assert_contains "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT" "\"agent\":\"claude\""
+    assert_json_valid "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT"
+}
+
+test_json_schedule_status_output() {
+    run_asb --json schedule --status
+    assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
+    assert_contains "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT" "\"cron\""
+    assert_contains "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT" "\"systemd\""
+    assert_json_valid "$ASB_LAST_OUTPUT"
+}
+
+run_test "json_list_output" test_json_list_output || exit 1
+run_test "json_history_output" test_json_history_output || exit 1
+run_test "json_verify_output" test_json_verify_output || exit 1
+run_test "json_schedule_status_output" test_json_schedule_status_output || exit 1
+
+exit 0
